what_it_does: This file is a model-card ledger entry that records metadata, acquisition rationale, intended use, and open questions for the downloaded Qwen/Qwen3.5-2B text-only base model.
observations: The model is a 2B parameter Qwen3.5 base model, not instruct-tuned.
It was downloaded specifically to avoid the vision-tower VRAM cost carried by the 0.8B, 4B, and 9B Qwen3.5 variants.
Status is downloaded but runtime is not-yet-served; it has never been test-loaded.
The entry lists open questions about vLLM loadability, chat-template applicability, usefulness without fine-tuning, throughput, and KV cache size.
It links to sibling model entries and the models hub via wikilinks.
standouts: Text-only variant chosen to drop vision-tower overhead.
Base model, so chat use would require fine-tuning or significant prompt engineering.
Downloaded but never test-loaded, making the entry largely speculative.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, date_added, and tags.
Uses wikilink-style internal references to related models and the hub.
Uses a markdown table for at-a-glance metadata.
Organizes content into source, fits these needs, tested performance, open questions, and connects-to sections.
concerns: Runtime is not-yet-served and no test-load data is present, so most claims are hypothetical.
The entry is dominated by open questions rather than verified facts.
notes: A speculative model-card for an untested 2B text-only base model, mainly justifying its download by the absence of a vision tower.
questions: What is the actual VRAM and throughput difference versus the 4B AWQ sibling in practice?
Will the base model load cleanly in vLLM and accept the patched chat template?
Is a 2B parameter model sufficient for the intended workloads without instruct tuning?
Does the text-only property actually yield measurable throughput gains over the AWQ-quantized 4B?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to document the Qwen3.5-2B model and assess its fit for high-throughput chat; actual content is mostly acquisition rationale plus open questions, consistent with the not-yet-served status.
apparent_themes: Cost and performance tradeoffs between vision-tower overhead and model capacity
intention_signals: The file is trying to capture why this specific small text-only base model was acquired and what still needs to be validated before it can be recommended for high-throughput chat serving.
novelty: It explicitly frames the 2B variant as a vision-tower-free alternative within a family where other sizes include vision towers, and flags that AWQ quantization may offset the expected VRAM advantage.
contribution: It preserves the rationale for selecting a text-only 2B variant, the list of validation steps needed before serving, and the explicit comparison framing against vision-enabled siblings.
summary_for_embedding: A model-card ledger entry for the downloaded but untested Qwen/Qwen3.5-2B BF16 text-only base model, documenting acquisition rationale, open validation questions, and links to sibling models.
intention_for_embedding: To record why the vision-tower-free 2B Qwen3.5 base model was pulled and what must be checked before it can be considered for high-throughput chat serving.
